title: Corollary c:39: removing the structural restriction T-S <= T_0-T via [BFG, Lemma 2.3]

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Where:** Corollary `c:39` in problem.tex.

`L26` is proved **only** under $T-S\le T_0-T$ (so that $\kappa=T_0-r\ge t-s$ in `c:key`). `c:39` upgrades to $T_0=T$ "immediately from [BFG, Lemma 2.3]" (taming singularities). At $T_0=T$, $\kappa=T-r\to0$ as $r\uparrow T$, putting the singular integral $\int p_{T-r}\sigma^{-1}$ at its worst near the diagonal.

**Gap.** This is not a one-liner. One must (i) state [BFG, Lemma 2.3] precisely, (ii) exhibit the blow-up rate of the `L26` constant as $T_0\downarrow T$, e.g. $\sim(T_0-T)^{-\alpha}$, and (iii) show $\alpha$ is small enough that summation over a dyadic decomposition of $[T,T_0]$ (or the $T_0\downarrow T$ limit) converges, yielding the unrestricted bounds. Verify the hypotheses of the taming-singularities lemma for these germs.
